detailed_explanation: The time period from sunrise to around noon. Used for greetings, daily routines, and scheduling.
example_sentences: ["朝ごはんを食べた - I ate breakfast","朝早く起きた - I woke up early","毎朝ジョギングする - I jog every morning"]
usage_notes: Very common time word. 朝ごはん (asagohan) specifically means breakfast.

detailed_explanation: Describes hot weather or high temperature. For objects/food use 熱い (atsui) with different kanji.
example_sentences: ["今日は暑いですね - It's hot today, isn't it?","夏はとても暑い - Summer is very hot","暑くなってきた - It's getting hot"]
usage_notes: Distinguish from 熱い (same reading) which is for hot objects, food, or fever.

detailed_explanation: Physical house or one's home. Used for where someone lives.
example_sentences: ["家に帰る - Go home","私の家は小さい - My house is small","家で勉強する - Study at home"]
usage_notes: Also written as いえ in hiragana for casual contexts.

detailed_explanation: Basic movement verb indicating motion away from the speaker. Irregular conjugation.
example_sentences: ["学校に行く - Go to school","明日行きます - I will go tomorrow","どこに行くの？ - Where are you going?"]
usage_notes: Group 1 (u-verb) with irregular te-form: 行って (itte). Not 行いて.

detailed_explanation: Candy, snacks, confectionery. Polite term for sweets and small food items.
example_sentences: ["お菓子を食べる - Eat snacks","お菓子屋さん - Candy shop","甘いお菓子が好き - I like sweet snacks"]
usage_notes: The お prefix makes it polite. Often given as gifts in Japan.
